title: Totally $\Delta$-modular IP Conjecture
statement_text: For every constant $\Delta \in \mathbb{Z}_{\geq 1}$, integer programs of the form $\max\{p^{\intercal}x : Mx \leq b,\, x \in \mathbb{Z}^{n}\}$, where $M$ is an integer matrix whose subdeterminants are all in $\{-\Delta, \dots, \Delta\}$, can be solved in polynomial time.
context_text: It is a notorious open question whether IPs with a totally $\Delta$-modular coefficient matrix $M$ can be solved in polynomial time for every fixed constant $\Delta$. Artmann, Weismantel and Zenklusen [AWZ17] established the $\Delta=2$ case, leading to the conjecture that polynomial-time solvability holds for every constant $\Delta$. The paper refers to this as the totally $\Delta$-modular IP conjecture and provides further evidence by solving it for the class of nearly totally unimodular matrices whose TU submatrix is the transpose of a network matrix.
paper_contribution: Answers the conjecture in the affirmative for the special case where $M$ is totally $\Delta$-modular and nearly totally unimodular with the unimodular submatrix $A$ (obtained by removing a constant number of rows and columns) being the transpose of a network matrix.
paper_title: Integer programs with nearly totally unimodular matrices: the cographic case
